---
name: agent-working-notes
description: Practical working notes for terminal agents. Turn discipline: identify deliverable → read verifier → read all source files → stub output immediately → copy before touching mutable resources → implement at 60% → fix failures literally → change structure after three same-shape failures. Spec literals are hard constraints including dates/paths/formats/whitespace/boundary conventions. Inspect pipeline intermediate output directly. Search environment systematically for missing values. Copy stateful resources before access; restore readability before parsing. Verify service config validity AND live connectivity. Measure reference match quantitatively, prefer deterministic. Test cancellation cleanup explicitly. Inspect unknown programs before executing. Minimal patches, stable clocks, trust-boundary validation. Read disk artifact before stopping.
---

# Agent Working Notes

Disk artifacts are the answer. Everything else is
intermediate work.

## Turn discipline

Start every task by identifying the deliverable:
its exact path, format, and success criteria. Read
the test or verifier script before writing anything —
it defines what passing looks like.

Read every source file the task references. Components
behave according to their actual code, not their
category name. A filter, a parser, a service
configuration — read the source before predicting
behavior.

Create the output file with stub content immediately.
Update it in place throughout the task.

Before touching any resource that may mutate on
access, copy it. Work on the copy.

Implement at 60% confidence. Run the check. Fix what
the failure names. Repeat.

Three failures with the same structure means the
structure is wrong. Investigate the root cause and
change approach.

One paragraph of planning, then action.

## Tools

Shell, Python, file I/O. Installed packages and
standard library only. No speculative imports.

## Spec compliance

Literals in the specification are hard constraints.
Reproduce dates, paths, names, and format characters
exactly. Tests compare against pinned values — runtime
expressions that produce the same value today may not
match the test's expected value.

When the spec provides sample output, match it
character for character: field separators, quoting
style, bracket types, spacing, trailing newlines.

Boundary semantics must be stated explicitly. When
counting intervals, name the convention you are using
and verify it matches the spec's intent.

## Pipeline inspection

When data flows through multiple components, the
output of each stage is the input to the next. To
understand what the final component receives, inspect
the intermediate output directly — run the upstream
component on your input and examine what it produces.

Reasoning about intermediate output from first
principles is unreliable. Measure it.

## Environment search

When a task requires information that is not in the
task description — a key, a credential, a
configuration value — search the environment
systematically before concluding it is absent.
Check all files in the working directory and parent
directories, environment variables, configuration
files, embedded strings in binaries, and comments
in source files.

## Stateful resource handling

Resources that maintain logs, journals, or history
may modify themselves when accessed. Identify this
property before issuing any command. Copy the
resource and all associated files to a working
location first. Operate on the copy.

When the goal is to recover data from a resource
the native tool cannot currently read, restore
readability first, then use the native tool to
extract data. Avoid manual binary parsing when a
working parser exists.

## Service startup verification

A service configuration script must leave the
service in a running, responding state. Verify this
explicitly: check that the configuration is valid,
start the service, and confirm it is accepting
connections before the script exits.

Specification literals in configuration — format
strings, directive values, port numbers — are tested
exactly. Match them.

## Reference matching

When producing output that must match a reference,
measure similarity quantitatively. Sample the
reference at representative points. Derive generation
parameters from those measurements. Verify the
similarity metric meets the required threshold before
finalizing.

Prefer deterministic approaches when operating under
resource constraints.

## Concurrent work

When implementing concurrent execution with
cancellation, verify that cleanup code runs for
every task that has started. Test this property
directly: trigger cancellation and confirm cleanup
ran for each started task.

## Unknown programs

Inspect before executing. File type, byte content,
and embedded strings are available without running
the program. Request usage information. Run with
minimal input. Observe all output channels.

## Hygiene

Minimal patches. Stable time sources. Validate at
trust boundaries. Comments only for non-obvious
reasoning.

## Before stopping

Read the artifact on disk. If it diverges from your
current understanding, update it. The final disk
state is the answer.
